checked.Cond1 (h<0 on X_u): X_u is the disk (x1-0.7)^2+(x2+0.7)^2<=0.09; on it max h = -0.0428 < 0. Margin thin but strictly negative; no unsafe point has h>=0.
checked.Cond2 (Nagumo, hdot>=0 on dC): hdot = -dH/dt. Along f: dH/dt = gradH . f = (-exp(-x1) z + sin(x1)^2)*z + z*(-sin(x1)^2) = -exp(-x1) z^2.  So hdot = exp(-x1)*z^2. Since exp(-x1)>0 and z^2>=0, hdot>=0 GLOBALLY (not just on the sampled boundary) -> Nagumo holds everywhere. This is the key fact pure sampling cannot certify; the algebraic cancellation is why the Refuter ring matters here.

refuter.counterexample_search: none found; both conditions hold symbolically.
